}
#main-wrap1 {

}
a:visited {
color: #660099;
}

}
a img {

/* Blog Header
----------------------------------------------- */

}

}
/* Posts
----------------------------------------------- */
h2.date-header {

}

}

}

text-decoration:none;
color: #333333;
}
h1.post-title {

.post-footer {

The first line of the post footer might only have floated text, so we need to give it a height.
The height comes from the post-footer line-height
*/
.post-footer-line-1 {
min-height:1.5em;
_height:1.5em;
}

}

}

}
a.comment-link {

<!-- script for increasing snippet length -->
<script type="text/javascript">
snippet_count = 200;

//<![CDATA[

function removeHtmlTag(strx,chop){
if(strx.indexOf("<")!=-1)
{
var snippet = strx.split("<");
for(var i=0;i<snippet.length;i++){
if(snippet[i].indexOf(">")!=-1){
snippet[i] = snippet[i].substring(snippet[i].indexOf(">")+1,snippet[i].length);
}
}
strx = snippet.join("");
}
chop = (chop < strx.length-1) ? chop : strx.length-2;
while(strx.charAt(chop-1)!=' ' && strx.indexOf(' ',chop)!=-1) chop++;
strx = strx.substring(0,chop-1);
return strx+'...';
}
function createSnippet(pID){
var div = document.getElementById(pID);
var summ = snippet_count;
var summary = '<div class="snippets">' + removeHtmlTag(div.innerHTML,summ) + '</div>';
div.innerHTML = summary;
}

}

@-webkit-keyframes div-down-slide {
  0% {
    left: 0vw;
    top: 0vh;
  }
  100% {
    top: calc(100vh - 21.6vh);
    left: calc(100vw - 38.7vh);
  }
}

@-webkit-keyframes bounce {
  0% {
    top: 100%;
    left: 100%;
  }
  100% {
    top: 15%;
    left: 0%;
  }
}

@keyframes div-down-slide {
  0% {
    top: 0%;
    left: 0%;
  }
  100% {
    top: calc(100vh - 21.6vh);
    left: calc(100vw - 38.7vh);
  }
}

@keyframes bounce {
  0% {
    top: 100%;
    left: 100%;
  }
  100% {
    top: 15%;
    left: 0%;
  }
}

.Viewport_To_Bottom {
  position: fixed;
  right: 10px;
  bottom: 15px;
  float: right;
  -webkit-animation: div-down-slide 0.7s;
  animation: div-down-slide 0.7s;
}

.Viewport_To_Top {
  position: fixed;
  -webkit-animation: bounce 0.7s;
  animation: bounce 0.7s;
}

/* rgba(255,255,255,0.30575980392156865) */
.vdo-carousel-indicators li {
  /* border: 1px solid white!important; */
  background: rgba(211, 211, 211, 0.4);
  width: 23%;
  height: 3px;
  border-radius: 2.5px;
  box-sizing: border-box;
  margin: 0px!important;
  text-align: left;
  padding: 0px!important;
  list-style: none;
}

.vdo-carousel-indicators li::before,
.vdo-carousel-indicators li::after{
  display: none!important;
}

.vdo-carousel-indicators li span {
  width: 0%;
  background: white;
  height: 3px;
  display: block;
  border-radius: 2.5px;
}

.vdo-stories-text h3,
.vdo-stories-text h3::after, 
.vdo-stories-text p {
  margin: 0px;
  padding: 0px;
  font-family: Raleway, sans-serif !important;
  background: #f2f2f2!important;
  color: #000000!important;
  font-size: 0.9em;
  line-height: calc(1em + 8px);
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 6px;
}
.vdo-stories-text h3::after{
  padding: 0px;
  font-size: 1em;
  line-height: calc(1em + 8px);
}

.vdo-titles h3,
.vdo-titles h3:visited,
.vdo-titles h3:active,
.vdo-titles h3:hover {
  color: black;
  /* font-size: 20px; */
}

.vdo-titles p,
.vdo-titles p:visited,
.vdo-titles p:active,
.vdo-titles p:hover {
  color: grey;
  text-decoration: none;
  font-size: 13px;
}

#vdo-stories-banner-frame {
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  border: none;
}

.vdo-stories-text {
  padding-top: 0px;
  position: relative;
  /* height: 70px;
  background-color: #fff;
  border-radius: 0px 0px 2px 2px; */
}

a.vdo-titles {
  text-decoration: none;
  margin: 0px;
}

div.vdo-story-title {
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 30px;
  /* transform: translate(50%, 100%); */
  width: 83%;
  left: 50px;
  text-align: left;
  overflow: hidden;
  transition: width 0.3s;
  border-radius: 3px;
  /* font-size: calc(16px + 6 * ((100vw - 320px) / 680)); */
}

div.vdo-story-title.collapsed {
  width: 0%!important;
}


div.vdo-story-title-wrapper {
  border-left: 5px solid #ff7f01;
}

div[id^="vdo_ai_div"] {
  direction: ltr!important;
}

/* The vdo-carousel-modal (background) */
.vdo-carousel-modal {
  display: none; /* Hidden by default */
  position: fixed; /* Stay in place */
  z-index: 999999; /* Sit on top */
  padding-top: 50px; /* Location of the box */
  left: 0;
  top: 0;
  width: 100%; /* Full width */
  height: 100%; /* Full height */
  overflow: auto; /* Enable scroll if needed */
  background-color: rgb(0,0,0); /* Fallback color */
  background-color: rgba(0,0,0,0.4); /* Black w/ opacity */
}


.vdo-modal-container{
  min-height: 120px;
}
.vdo-modal-container, .vdo-modal-button-container{
  text-align: center;
}
/* The Close Button */
.vdo-carousel-close {
  color: #aaaaaa;
  float: right;
  font-size: 28px;
  font-weight: bold;
}

.vdo-carousel-close:hover,
.vdo-carousel-close:focus {
  color: #000;
  text-decoration: none;
  cursor: pointer;
}


#vdo-already-member{
  color: #000;
  background-color: #ccc;
  margin-top: 5px;
}
#vdo-already-member:hover{
  background-color: #ddd;
}
/* Chrome, Safari, Edge, Opera */
/* .vdo-carousel-modal-content input::-webkit-outer-spin-button,
.vdo-carousel-modal-content input::-webkit-inner-spin-button {
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  margin: 0;
}


.vdo-carousel-modal-content input[type=number] {
  -moz-appearance: textfield; 
}*/
.vdo-alert {
  padding: 20px;
  background-color: #f44336;
  color: white;
  opacity: 1;
  transition: opacity 0.6s;
  margin-bottom: 15px;
  position: fixed;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  text-align: center;
  width: 100%;
}

.vdo-alert.success {display: none ;background-color: #04AA6D; text-transform: uppercase;}
.vdo-alert.error {display: none ;background-color: red; text-transform: uppercase;}

/* card contain styles */
.vdo-carousel-modal-content {
  display: flex;
  max-width: 550px;
  margin: auto;
  box-shadow: 0px 10px 30px rgb(192, 192, 192);
  margin-top: 5rem;
  border-radius: 0.3rem;
  background-color: #fff;
}

/* styles for left part in container */
.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left {
  padding: 2rem;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .vdo-title strong{
  font-size: 2rem;
  color: #595959;
  /* text-transform: capitalize; */
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}
.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .vdo-title p{
  font-size: 1.5rem;
  color: #595959;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .form-input-control label,.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .date-and-cvv label{
  color: rgb(153, 153, 153);
  font-size: 0.8rem;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .form-input-control {
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .form-input-control input {
  width: 100%;
  border: none;
  font-size: 1.02rem;
  height: 100%;
  padding-bottom: 0.3rem;
  color: #202020;
}
.vdo-carousel-modal-content input{
  outline: none;
}
.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .form-input-control input::placeholder {
  color: #bbbbbb;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .form-input-control .vdo-input {
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(199, 199, 199);
  margin-top: 0.3rem;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .date-and-cvv .common {
  width: 48%;
  float: left;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .date-and-cvv {
  /* display: flex; */
  justify-content: space-between;
  border: none;
  font-size: 1.02rem;
  height: 100%;
  padding-bottom: 0.3rem;
  color: #202020;
  margin: 0 0 4rem 0;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .date-and-cvv .cvv {
  position: relative;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .date-and-cvv .cvv .icon {
  position: absolute;
  top: 0;
  right: 0;
  color: #bbbbbb;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .date-and-cvv input {
  border: none;
  font-size: 1.01rem;
  /* height: 100%; */
  width: 100%;
  padding-bottom: 0.3rem;
  color: #202020;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(199, 199, 199);
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .date-and-cvv input::placeholder {
  color: #bbbbbb;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}
.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .save-btn, .vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .subscribed-btn {

  width: 100%;
  position: relative;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left button {
  background-color: rgb(118, 201, 149);
  width: 100%;
  padding: 0.9rem;
  border: none;
  box-shadow: none;
  border-radius: 0.1rem;
  color: #f2f2f2;
  font-size: 1rem;
  text-transform: capitalize;
  letter-spacing: 1px;
  cursor: pointer;
}
.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .subscribed-btn button {
  background-color: #ccc;
  color: #000;
}
.vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .back-btn button {
  background-color: transparent;
  color: #000;
}
.back-btn{
  display: none;
}
.vdo-error{
  color: red;;
  font-size: 12px;
  display: inline;
}

/* styles for right part of the container */

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .right {
  background: rgb(116, 110, 231);
  background: linear-gradient(
    297deg,
    rgba(116, 110, 231, 1) 0%,
    rgba(0, 212, 255, 1) 100%
  );
  width: 50%;
  padding: 1rem 2rem 2rem 2rem;
  position: relative;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .right .close-icon {
  text-align: right;
  color: #f2f2f2;
  font-size: 2rem;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .right .close-icon span {
  cursor: pointer;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .right .card {
  background-color: rgba(0, 212, 255, 0.1);
  position: absolute;
  border: 2px solid #f2f2f2;
  width: 60%;
  border-radius: 0.5rem;
  padding: 1rem 1.5rem;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .right .card .brand {
  text-align: right;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .right .card .brand .icon {
  color: #f2f2f2;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .right .card .vdo-card-number {
  color: #f2f2f2;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  margin-bottom: 1.4rem;
}

.vdo-carousel-modal-content .right .card .additional-details {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  color: #f2f2f2;
}

#vdo-form-input-email, #vdo-form-input-name, #vdo-form-input-card-no, #vdo-form-date-cvv{
  display: none;
}

/* styles for diffrent media */

@media only screen and (max-width: 692px) {
  .vdo-carousel-modal-content {
    max-width: 90%;
  }

  .vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left {
    width: 100%;
  }
}

@media only screen and (max-width: 440px) {
  .vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left button {
    font-size: 0.8rem;
  }
  .vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .vdo-title strong{
    font-size: 1.1rem;
  }
  .vdo-carousel-modal-content .vdo-left .vdo-title p{
    font-size: 1rem;
  }
}


@media only screen and (max-width: 600px) {
  .vdo-stories-text h3, .vdo-stories-text p {
    font-size: 0.9em!important;
  }

}

.center-floating{
  left: 0px;
  right: 0px;
  z-index: 10;
  padding: 0px!important;
  width: 100% !important;
  position: fixed !important;
  background-color: #212424;
  transition: top .6s linear !important;
}

.center-floating .vdo-carousel-inner{
  margin: 0 auto;
}

.center-floating #vdo_stories_ai_cross{
  right: 5px !important;
  top: 1px !important;
}

<div style="color: black;">
<br></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">To develop fix connectivity you need to agree upon which FIX protocol version are you going to follow, since many fix protocol version exists e.g. FIX4.0, FIX4.1, FIX4.2 and FIX4.4 even newer version are available but most firm use FIX 4.2 as there preferred FIX connectivity solution or you can say still most widely used version is FIX 4.2, many firms, clients, hedge funds, pension funds still use it for online trading but even after the launch of more advance version it still remains the popular one. On advanced side FIX 4.4 is getting popular.</span></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<br></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">In this short discussion I am highlighting some of the <b>difference between FIX 4.2 and FIX 4.4</b>. Two most popular FIX protocol versions you can check FIX specification for more detail and some other difference which exits. FIX specification are available on <a href="http://www.fixprotocol.org/">http://www.fixprotocol.org</a>  you can download pdf version of these specification and can look before developing your FIX connectivity.</span></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<br></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">There are many differences between these two versions of fix protocol, some of them I am highlighting here.</span></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">1) There was tag called ExecTransType (tag 20) which was there in FIX 4.2 and now merged to ExecType tag 150 into FIX4.4, this tag used to define the nature of execution received from the exchange or broker.</span></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">2) Replaced the use of the QuantityType (tag 465) field with new QtyType (tag 854) which contains a re-organized/consolidated set of values.</span></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">3) Removed various Settlement Instructions-related fields.</span></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">4) Added "Quote Response" to list of messages for which Quote Status Report message is the appropriate response.</span></div>
<br>
<div style="display: table; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">
<script async="" src="//pagead2.googlesyndication.com/pagead/js/adsbygoogle.js"></script>
<!--336x280_Middle_Of_Post-->
<ins class="adsbygoogle" data-ad-client="ca-pub-5808379456926196" data-ad-slot="6534721145" style="display: inline-block; height: 280px; width: 336px;" data-adsbygoogle-status="done"><div id="aswift_2_host" style="border-width: medium; border-style: none; border-color: currentcolor; border-image: none; height: 280px; width: 336px; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; position: relative; visibility: visible; background-color: transparent; display: inline-block;"></div></ins>
<script>

</script>
</div>
<br>
<div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">Establishing FIX connectivity is challenging if it involves custom fix tags, these are the tags that are not defined by FIX protocol but used by many firms or between client and broker for carrying some extra information that is agreed upon that. </span></div><div style="color: black;"><span style="font-family: Verdana;"><br></span></div><div style="color: black;"><span style="font-family: Verdana;">FIX protocol allows you to define custom FIX tags for your FIX connectivity until and unless they are not conflicting with any other FIX tags. Generally, firms use tags above 10000 or 5000 to define these custom FIX tags.</span></div><div style="color: black;"><span style="font-family: Verdana;"><br></span></div><div style="color: black;"><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhDklasUTKuwaXr83mM-5K_M3-EJfH1hUGHaNGadv_95EePtosnk8iTt75diiL6EJ6LzaOJtCMCz6n7feBFoibx_GO4AmdpfXXG40ZrRqPPYhh230_4rUlDumG_k1xVSvkC1V43r2lkUs0/s1078/FIX+Engine+Session+Working.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img alt="Difference between FIX 4.2 vs FIX 4.4 Protocol? <div style="color: black;">
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">There are many other changes that are available in the release notes of fix 4.4 <a href="http://www.fixprotocol.org/">http://www.fixprotocol.org</a>, I will add some more as I come to know which is used in day trading more often.</span></div>
<div style="color: black;">
<div style="float: left;">
</div>
</div>
<br>
<span style="font-family: Verdana;">I have documented my discussion on my <a href="http://javarevisited.blogspot.com/search/label/FIX%20protocol%20tutorial">FIX protocol tutorial </a>series, please check for more information on different areas of FIX protocol and FIX connectivity e.g <a href="http://javarevisited.blogspot.com/2011/01/fix-protocol-tutorial-fix-session-is.html">FIX Session management</a>, FIX application management, or <a href="http://javarevisited.blogspot.com/2010/12/fix-protocol-interview-questions.html">FIX Interview questions</a>. </span><br><br>

<dt class="comment-author anon-comment-icon" id="c8551346822203542066">

<dd class="comment-body" id="Blog1_cmt-8551346822203542066">
<p>
Hi Anonymous here are your answers , though you could ask that into one comment instead of three :)<br><br>1. Generally custom tag in FIX Protocol is something every investment bank or equity broker or any other FIRM use to carry firm specific tags which is useful to cater different FIX clients and also to carry booking , settlement and some other specific information. finance domain is so huge and every client is different which trades with broker are different. normally after 10000 tags are used for custom tag but some time 6000-9000 tag number is also used for same.<br><br>2. yes if a FIX engine supports FIX4.2 version than it can support FIX4.0 but that is not hard and fast rule it depends upon how FIX engine is implemented. some professional FIX Engine e.g Cameron FIX Engine, NYFIX Appia etc supports multiple version of FIX Protocol.<br><br>3. For Downloading FIX Protocol Specification you can visit fixportocol.org site , the link is in this post itself if you have read it. every FIX protocol version has different specification e.g FIX4.0 or FIX4.2 so make sure you download correct version also its worth looking at release notes and errata of different FIX versions.
</p>
</dd>

<dd class="comment-body" id="Blog1_cmt-6155326104383386771">
<p>
Hi Anonymous,<br>Sorry but i don't have any FIX protocol testing script with me but I can suggest you to test New , Mod and Cancel for different OrdType (tag 40) and TimeInForce (tag 59)  combination. you can also test various modification scenario mentioned in FIX protocol specification document. Brokers normally don't support all the OrdType , its better to look there ROE Document (Rules for Engagement) to actually understand what they support and what they not. you can also make FIX testing scenario in xls for your future reference.<br><br>Thanks<br>Javin
</p>
</dd>

<dd class="comment-body" id="Blog1_cmt-4906309914784993702">
<p>
Hi Anonymous 1,<br><br>You can start with FIX4.0 because its the oldest popular version and many clients and systems are still using this in FIX messaging layer. then you can move ahead with FIX4.2 and FIX4.4 which is more feature rich as compared to FIX4.0 and gained popularity with advanced trading systems.<br>Please refer FIX protocol specification for details.<br><br>Thanks<br>Javin
</p>
</dd>

<dd class="comment-body" id="Blog1_cmt-2789389599666794873">
<p>
Hi Raj,<br><br>you are right in FIX4.0 OrdStatus (fix tag 39) is used to denote execution type also which is later segregated into tag 150 (ExecType) form FIX4.2 so any FIX engine logic written based on OrdStatus will not work in FIX4.2 and you need to write it again to represent correct status of order in FIX messaging.<br><br>Thanks<br>Javin
</p>
</dd>

<dd class="comment-body" id="Blog1_cmt-5504996226338348286">
<p>
buddy, yesterday I went though one interview with one of the FIX broker in Europe. interview is mostly on java but they did ask my experience about FIX , as I said I have some experience they asked me "What is difference between tag 11 (ClOrdID) and tag 38 (OrderID) ? dude do you know?
</p>

<span class="comment-timestamp">
<a href="https://javarevisited.blogspot.com/2011/01/difference-between-fix-42-vs-fix-44-in.html?showComment=1296889207118#c5504996226338348286" title="comment permalink">

</a>
<span class="item-control blog-admin pid-2066954516">
<a class="comment-delete" href="https://www.blogger.com/comment/delete/8712770457197348465/5504996226338348286" title="Delete Comment">

<dt class="comment-author anon-comment-icon" id="c3536801155577169315">
<a name="c3536801155577169315"></a>
Anonymous
said...
</dt>
<dd class="comment-body" id="Blog1_cmt-3536801155577169315">
<p>
Raj, Tag 55 is the exchange symbol typically.  Tag 48 and 22 are used for the various security numbering schemes such as CUSIP, ISIN SEDOL etc.<br><br>Richard, Tag 11 is the order ID that is sent you from the client or the one you send out to the other counterpary.  Tag 37 OrderID is the internal orderID you assign in your system, this is sent back to the client as your own unique internal order id. <br><br>If you want to play with FIX take a look at QuickfixJ for Java and quickfix c++.<br>Fix is actually on 5.0 SP2 now and this provides many advanced trading functions for exoctic trading products.
</p>
</dd>

<p>
Hi Anonymous, Thanks for answering Raj's question. Symbology lookup is an important point of order processing cycle because many exchanges needs the symbol in form or Exchange ticker and most of clients prefer to send either RIC or BET (Bloomberg ticker) , Symbology or Product lookup does this translation based upon exchange requirement.
</p>

</p>

<script type="text/javascript">
  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
    function convertLink(url) {
      // ✅ New Udemy deep link pattern (Account 1)
      if (url.indexOf('click.linksynergy.com/deeplink') !== -1 &&
          url.indexOf('udemy.com') !== -1 &&
          url.indexOf('id=JVFxdTr9V80') !== -1) {
        return url.replace(
          /^https:\/\/click\.linksynergy\.com\/deeplink\?id=[^&]+&mid=39197&murl=/,
          'https://trk.udemy.com/c/3294490/3262185/39854?u='
        );

      // ✅ New Udemy deep link pattern (Account 2)
      } else if (url.indexOf('click.linksynergy.com/deeplink') !== -1 &&
                 url.indexOf('udemy.com') !== -1 &&
                 url.indexOf('id=CuIbQrBnhiw') !== -1) {
        return url.replace(
          /^https:\/\/click\.linksynergy\.com\/deeplink\?id=[^&]+&mid=39197&murl=/,
          'https://trk.udemy.com/c/3294490/3262185/39854?u='
        );

      // ✅ OLD Udemy fs-bin pattern
      } else if (url.indexOf('click.linksynergy.com/fs-bin/click') !== -1 &&
                 url.indexOf('udemy.com') !== -1) {
        var match = url.match(/RD_PARM1=([^&]+)/);
        if (match && match[1]) {
          var decodedUdemyUrl = decodeURIComponent(match[1]);
          return 'https://trk.udemy.com/c/3294490/3262185/39854?u=' +
                 encodeURIComponent(decodedUdemyUrl);
        }
        return url;

      // ✅ Direct Udemy.com link → convert to affiliate link
      } else if (url.indexOf('udemy.com/course/') !== -1) {
        return 'https://trk.udemy.com/c/3294490/3262185/39854?u=' +
               encodeURIComponent(url);

      // ✅ Coursera - Specializations
      } else if (url.indexOf('coursera.org/specializations') !== -1) {
        return url.replace(
          'https://www.coursera.org/specializations/',
          'https://coursera.pxf.io/c/3294490/1164545/14726?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.coursera.org%2Fspecializations%2F'
        );

      // ✅ Coursera - Individual Course
      } else if (url.indexOf('coursera.org/learn') !== -1) {
        return url.replace(
          'https://www.coursera.org/learn/',
          'https://coursera.pxf.io/c/3294490/1164545/14726?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.coursera.org%2Flearn%2F'
        );

      // ✅ Coursera - Professional Certificates
      } else if (url.indexOf('coursera.org/professional-certificates') !== -1) {
        return url.replace(
          'https://www.coursera.org/professional-certificates/',
          'https://coursera.pxf.io/c/3294490/1164545/14726?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.coursera.org%2Fprofessional-certificates%2F'
        );
      }

      // Default: return unchanged
      return url;
    }

    function processLinks() {
      document.querySelectorAll('a').forEach(function (link) {
        var originalHref = link.href;
        var newHref = convertLink(originalHref);
        if (newHref !== originalHref) {
          link.href = newHref;
        }
      });
    }

    processLinks();
  });
</script>
